input.command:
for file in ./src/index.rs ./src/parser/c_lang.rs ./src/parser/cpp.rs ./src/parser/csharp.rs ./src/parser/go.rs ./src/parser/java.rs ./src/parser/javascript.rs ./src/parser/python.rs ./src/parser/ruby.rs ./src/parser/rust_lang.rs ./src/parser/treesitter.rs ./src/parser/typescript.rs ./src/query/index.rs; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    echo "=== $file ==="
    grep -n "parse_file" "$file" | head -5
  fi
done

cat > /tmp/check_parse_file.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

# Files to check (from our search results)
files=(
  "./src/index.rs"
  "./src/parser/c_lang.rs"
  "./src/parser/cpp.rs"
  "./src/parser/csharp.rs"
  "./src/parser/go.rs"
  "./src/parser/java.rs"
  "./src/parser/javascript.rs"
  "./src/parser/python.rs"
  "./src/parser/ruby.rs"
  "./src/parser/rust_lang.rs"
  "./src/parser/treesitter.rs"
  "./src/parser/typescript.rs"
  "./src/query/index.rs"
)

for file in "${files[@]}"; do
  if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    # Use grep to find all lines with parse_file and mark the context
    matches=$(grep -n "parse_file" "$file" | grep -v "^[0-9]*:\s*//" | grep -v "^[0-9]*:\s*\*" | grep -v "fn parse_file")
    
    if [ ! -z "$matches" ]; then
      # Check if any line contains an actual call (has an opening paren after parse_file)
      if grep -q "parse_file\s*(" "$file"; then
        echo "$file"
      fi
    fi
  fi
done
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/check_parse_file.sh
/tmp/check_parse_file.sh

trace.result_preview:
572:        // finding where `parse_file(...)` called as `crate::parser::
573:        // treesitter::parse_file(...)` from src/index.rs was missed.

input.command:
# Double-check each file to make sure we have actual function calls
for file in ./src/index.rs ./src/parser/c_lang.rs ./src/parser/cpp.rs ./src/parser/csharp.rs ./src/parser/go.rs ./src/parser/java.rs ./src/parser/javascript.rs ./src/parser/python.rs ./src/parser/ruby.rs ./src/parser/rust_lang.rs ./src/parser/treesitter.rs ./src/parser/typescript.rs; do
  echo "=== $file ==="
  # Find lines with parse_file( that are not in comments or function definitions
  grep -n "parse_file\s*(" "$file" | grep -v "^\s*//" | head -3
done

# Get all files with parse_file( calls
files=$(grep -l "parse_file\s*(" \
  ./src/index.rs \
  ./src/parser/c_lang.rs \
  ./src/parser/cpp.rs \
  ./src/parser/csharp.rs \
  ./src/parser/go.rs \
  ./src/parser/java.rs \
  ./src/parser/javascript.rs \
  ./src/parser/python.rs \
  ./src/parser/ruby.rs \
  ./src/parser/rust_lang.rs \
  ./src/parser/treesitter.rs \
  ./src/parser/typescript.rs \
  2>/dev/null)

# For each file, check if it has actual calls (not just the definition on line 13)
result_files=()
for file in $files; do
  # Count lines with parse_file( that are not the definition
  call_count=$(grep -n "parse_file\s*(" "$file" | grep -v "^13:" | wc -l)
  
  if [ "$call_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    result_files+=("$file")
  fi
done

# Sort and output
printf '%s\n' "${result_files[@]}" | sort

# Final verification: for each file, show that we have actual calls
for file in ./src/index.rs ./src/parser/c_lang.rs ./src/parser/cpp.rs ./src/parser/csharp.rs ./src/parser/go.rs ./src/parser/java.rs ./src/parser/javascript.rs ./src/parser/python.rs ./src/parser/ruby.rs ./src/parser/rust_lang.rs ./src/parser/treesitter.rs ./src/parser/typescript.rs; do
  has_call=$(grep "parse_file\s*(" "$file" | grep -v "pub fn parse_file" | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*//" | wc -l)
  if [ "$has_call" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "$file: YES ($has_call calls)"
  fi
done | sort

input.command:
# Create JSON array of files with parse_file calls, sorted lexicographically
files=(
  "./src/index.rs"
  "./src/parser/c_lang.rs"
  "./src/parser/cpp.rs"
  "./src/parser/csharp.rs"
  "./src/parser/go.rs"
  "./src/parser/java.rs"
  "./src/parser/javascript.rs"
  "./src/parser/python.rs"
  "./src/parser/ruby.rs"
  "./src/parser/rust_lang.rs"
  "./src/parser/treesitter.rs"
  "./src/parser/typescript.rs"
)

# Remove ./ prefix for cleaner output and sort
printf '%s\n' "${files[@]}" | sed 's|^\./||' | sort | jq -R -s -c 'split("\n") | map(select(length > 0))'

# Verify a few files have real calls
echo "=== src/index.rs - should show actual call ==="
sed -n '34,36p' ./src/index.rs

echo ""
echo "=== src/parser/treesitter.rs - definition on line 13, actual call on 271 ==="
sed -n '13p' ./src/parser/treesitter.rs
echo "---"
sed -n '270,272p' ./src/parser/treesitter.rs

echo ""
echo "=== src/parser/python.rs - should show actual call in test ==="
sed -n '816,817p' ./src/parser/python.rs
